research_tasks.task_title: Replot the figure encoded in the replot-ready Excel dataset for the 2D non-Hermitian topolectrical circuit
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided legacy Excel file, inspect the sheet structure and reconstruct the figure panel(s) associated with this replot-ready dataset. Your goal is to identify the plotted variables, determine whether the file contains spectra, voltage maps, or frequency-response curves, and reproduce the corresponding publication-quality figure as closely as possible from the data alone. Begin by loading the `.xls` file with a legacy-compatible reader, listing sheet names, column headers, and sample rows. Then determine the figure mapping by matching the data structure to the likely 2D topolectrical-circuit measurements: circuit-Laplacian/admittance spectra under different boundary conditions, resonance voltage distributions, or voltage-vs-frequency traces at selected lattice sites. Once identified, generate the appropriate plot(s) with clear axis labels, legends, and panel annotations. If the file contains multiple sheets or multiple plot types, treat them as subpanels of one figure and reproduce them together in a single multi-panel layout.
execution_steps: Step_0: Load the `.xls` file using a parser that supports the old Excel format (for example, `xlrd` or a conversion workflow to `.xlsx`).
Step_1: Inspect all sheet names, dimensions, column headers, and a few representative rows to identify the measured variables and any figure-panel organization.
Step_2: Determine whether the data correspond to spectra, heatmaps, or response curves; infer the matching paper figure from the data structure and the 2D topolectrical-circuit context.
Step_3: Clean and reshape the data as needed for plotting, preserving any labels for boundary conditions, node positions, frequency, voltage amplitude, or eigenvalue/admittance values.
Step_4: Recreate the figure with publication-style formatting, including axis labels, legends, colorbars if needed, and panel labels if multiple subplots are present.
Step_5: Save the final plot(s) and provide a brief note describing how the file structure maps to the reproduced figure.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A publication-style replot of the figure encoded in the Excel file, likely corresponding to one of the 2D circuit result panels from the paper. The output should include the correctly identified data visualization with labeled axes and any necessary subpanels or legends.
